ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for wok-fried sliced beef noodle; closest parent 'noodle dish' (FOODON_00002700) used as category anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP for stir-fried noodles with meat used as closest parent; no specific code for wok-fried sliced beef noodle exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of wok-fried sliced beef noodle is approximately 350 g, typically presented as a single plate with noodles, sliced beef, and vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Beef Fried Noodles (Char Kway Teow / Fried Noodle composite) — HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo entry for 'wok-fried sliced beef noodle' was found. Values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo fried noodle dish entries (beef fried noodle category) cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy data for cooked egg noodles (NDB 20113) and cooked beef (NDB 13365). Per-100g values represent a composite of approximately 55% cooked egg noodles, 25% sliced cooked beef, 10% vegetables, and 10% sauce/oil, consistent with typical Singapore restaurant preparation. Energy calculated using Atwater factors.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — egg noodles and stir-fried noodle analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for wok-fried sliced beef noodle. GI of 50 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for egg noodles (GI ~46–54, mean ~50) and stir-fried noodle dishes. The protein and fat content of the beef and sauce components further attenuate glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 68.25) / 100 = 34.1, based on 350 g serving containing approximately 68.25 g available carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1680 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium load from soy sauce, oyster sauce, and dark soy sauce; a single serving may provide approximately 70–84% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~34 per serving) from egg noodle carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish in controlled portions, as the moderate glycemic load of approximately 34 per standard serving can raise postprandial blood glucose; pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables and reducing portion size to 200–250 g may help moderate glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from beef and cooking oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be cautious with this dish due to its combined sodium (~1680 mg per serving) and saturated fat (~6.65 g per serving) content; choosing leaner beef cuts, reducing sauce quantity, and limiting portion size are recommended strategies.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wok-Fried Sliced Beef Noodle (牛肉炒面) is a popular Chinese-influenced noodle dish widely served in Singapore hawker centres, coffee shops, and casual restaurants. The dish consists of egg noodles stir-fried at high heat in a wok with thinly sliced beef, bean sprouts, spring onions, and garlic, seasoned with a combination of soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and oyster sauce. The high-heat wok technique imparts a characteristic smoky 'wok hei' flavour and produces noodles with a slightly charred, chewy texture. The sliced beef is typically marinated in soy sauce, sesame oil, and cornstarch before cooking to ensure tenderness. Nutritionally, a standard 350 g restaurant serving provides approximately 567 kcal, 29.8 g protein, 20.3 g fat, and 68.3 g carbohydrate. The dish is energy-dense and high in sodium due to the sauce components, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low GI) based on egg noodle data, though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 34. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans, and contains common allergens including gluten, soy, egg, and shellfish derivatives from oyster sauce. It is a culturally significant dish in Singapore's Chinese culinary heritage and is commonly ordered as a main meal at lunch or dinner.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry was found for wok-fried sliced beef noodle as a named dish. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo fried noodle dish categories and USDA SR Legacy ingredient-level data using a defensible composite model. GI is estimated from University of Sydney egg noodle data. Confidence is medium rather than high because exact dish-level measurement data from an approved primary source was not available.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy source data for this dish category. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B vitamins, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy ingredient-level data and are approximate. The dish is served at Viet'Spice - Hương vị Việt - Circular Road on field survey data at SGD 18.90, suggesting a premium restaurant preparation that may differ from standard hawker centre portions. Halal status is marked false as the restaurant name and context do not confirm halal certification; consumers requiring halal-certified food should verify with the specific restaurant. The flat aliases list and aliases_by_language object have been verified for consistency: all entries in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
